$comment: Glossary terms used in pronominal_verbs explanations not yet in data/glossary.json. Project author reviews and merges. Checked against glossary.json v3 (has clitic, clitic cluster, DOP, IOP, reflexive/subject/stressed pronoun; none of the three below).

pronominal verb.short: A verb that carries fixed little pronouns as part of its very meaning: andarsene (to leave), farcela (to manage), cavarsela (to get by), volerci (to take/require).
pronominal verb.long: A pronominal verb welds one or more clitic pronouns onto a verb so tightly that they become part of the dictionary word. The attached pronouns no longer point at anything you could name. The reflexive piece still conjugates for the person (me la cavo, te la cavi, se la cava), while the other clitics (la, ne, ci) stay fixed. The meaning is usually idiomatic and not deducible from the parts, which is why these verbs are learnt whole.
pronominal verb.needed_for: The four-beat named term in nearly every pronominal_verbs explanation.

frozen clitic.short: A clitic that is a permanent part of a pronominal verb and never changes or refers to a real object: the la in cavarsela, the ne in andarsene.
frozen clitic.long: In a pronominal verb some clitics are frozen: they stand for nothing and do not move or agree the way ordinary object pronouns do. The la in me la cavo is frozen (there is no 'it' being managed), which is why in compound tenses the participle agrees with that la as an invariable feminine (ce l'ho fatta, se l'è cavata) rather than with the subject. Contrast the reflexive piece, which is not frozen and does conjugate with the person.
frozen clitic.needed_for: Explains the invariable feminine participle in the -sela family and the fixed ce/ne shapes across the topic.

lexicalised.short: Absorbed into a word as a fixed unit, so the parts no longer carry their own separate meaning.
lexicalised.long: When a combination becomes lexicalised, speakers store and use it as a single item rather than rebuilding it from its pieces each time. The ci in volerci and the ne in fregarsene are lexicalised: you cannot swap them out or read them literally as 'there' or 'of it'. Lexicalisation is exactly why pronominal verbs sit in the dictionary as whole verbs with their own meaning.
lexicalised.needed_for: Used to explain why the clitics in these verbs cannot be parsed literally.
